what_input_feature: Same deep DTD structure reaching xmlHashLookup3 with an aliasing dict-interned name3, requiring sustained mutation pressure on the structured corpus seeds.
why_winner_satisfies: minimizer uses PowerSchedule None (energy from intrinsic calibration only), so the structured DTD seeds keep earning mutations regardless of how often their coverage region was hit, resolving 9/10.
why_loser_doesnt: fast is identical except its FAST AFLFast schedule multiplies energy by an n_fuzz log2 factor that damps frequently-hit (saturated) paths to 0.4x; the hash-lookup path is exercised often, so fast starves the very seeds that could build the deep structure (resolved 2/10, blocked 8/10).
mechanism_attribution: the AFLFast rarity power schedule (fast) reweights energy by region hit-frequency and damps the saturated hash-lookup path; minimizer's frequency-agnostic calibrated energy avoids this starvation — the aflfast_rarity delta is what separates them.

what_input_feature: A DTD attribute declaration whose value is parsed as NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION (xmlValidateAttributeValueInternal cases XML_ATTRIBUTE_NMTOKENS/XML_ATTRIBUTE_ENUMERATION at valid.c:3875) so xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal is invoked, AND whose value string, after the name-char and 0x20-blank loops, ends on a non-zero character so that valid.c:3807 `if (val != 0)` evaluates TRUE. The required input is structural (valid XML+DTD prefix with an ENUMERATION/NMTOKENS attribute), not a specific magic literal; both arms share the same XML/DTD scaffold bytes.
why_winner_satisfies: Minimizer reaches the blocker's true side (L3807 W T=12 F=0) and resolves it in 9/10 trials. Its calibrated power schedule (PowerSchedule None, intrinsic-calibration-only energy) keeps allocating havoc/token mutations to the seeds carrying the XML+DTD ENUMERATION scaffold, so it eventually mutates a value that lands val!=0 at line 3807.
why_loser_doesnt: Fast is byte-for-byte the same fuzzer except for the AFLFast FAST power schedule; it blocks 8/10 (loser blocked=8, L3807 L T=0 F=90 — never takes the true side). The FAST n_fuzz-based energy formula damps seeds whose coverage bucket has been hit campaign-wide, starving the deep attribute-validation path of mutation budget before val!=0 is produced.
mechanism_attribution: The winner is enabled by minimizer's calibrated energy schedule (PowerSchedule None: per-seed mutation budget derived only from intrinsic calibration perf_score). The loser fast applies the AFLFast n_fuzz rarity damping that throttles already-exercised buckets, so the single-technique delta (aflfast_rarity) costs fast the mutation budget needed to push a seed to val!=0 at valid.c:3807. This is a scheduling/energy divergence, not comparison-solving or byte-content.
